_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of principles and processes for providing customer and personal services. This includes customer needs assessment, meeting quality standards for services, and evaluation of customer satisfaction.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of self-service data platform design principles, including data marketplace concepts, data catalog usability, access request workflows, and user experience considerations that drive adoption.
comments: Partial overlap: WIDAI covers internal 'customer' service (data consumers as customers of data platforms). O*NET covers broader customer service. The intersection is in needs assessment and service quality. Secondary: OP-S-004 (service catalog management).
_comment_atlas_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: Moderate similarity (nist band: roughly as similar as dissimilar). The STS score indicates strong partial overlap — the intersection between these two concept spaces is substantial and semantically rich.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: F1 of 0.8801 indicates moderate token-level semantic alignment. Precision and Recall are balanced, indicating roughly symmetric semantic coverage between the two descriptions. For an Intersects relationship, the P/R balance indicates which direction the overlap tilts — whether the FDE captures more of the KSA's content or vice versa.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: Neutral-dominant (99.69%) — the model finds the descriptions related but neither implying nor contradicting each other. Neutral is the expected NLI outcome for Intersects relationships — the descriptions share conceptual territory without one implying the other.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: The bi-encoder score (0.4738) is close to the STS cross-encoder (0.5260). The small gap (0.05) suggests the semantic relationship is largely capturable through independent encoding — the descriptions share enough surface-level semantic features that cross-attention adds only marginal signal.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Low lexical overlap (10.53% of tokens shared). Some shared vocabulary exists (likely common functional words or broadly-used domain terms) but the bulk of each description uses distinct terminology.
